heading: Sale or receipt of stolen vehicles
label: 2313 Sale or receipt of stolen vehicles
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 408 (, ; , §§ 2, 3, ).Oct. 29, 1919, ch. 89, § 441 Stat. 325Sept. 24, 1945, ch. 38359 Stat. 536Section constitutes the fourth sentence of said , U.S.C., 1940 ed.section 408 of title 18Definitions of “aircraft,” “motor vehicle,” and “interstate or foreign commerce,” which constituted the second sentence of said section 408, are incorporated in sections 10 and 2311 of this title.The third sentence of said section 408, relating to transporting stolen aircraft or motor vehicles, is incorporated in .section 2312 of this titleThe first sentence of said section 408, providing the short title, and the fifth sentence thereof, relating to venue, were omitted. (See reviser’s note under .)section 2312 of this titleMinor changes were made in phraseology.

Amendments2006—Subsec. (a). substituted “motor vehicle, vessel, or aircraft” for “motor vehicle or aircraft”.Pub. L. 109–1771992—Subsec. (a). substituted “fined under this title or imprisoned not more than 10 years” for “fined not more than $5,000 or imprisoned not more than five years”.Pub. L. 102–5191990— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a) and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 101–6471984— inserted “possesses,” after “receives,” and substituted “which has crossed a State or United States boundary after being stolen,” for “moving as, or which is a part of, or which constitutes interstate or foreign commerce,”.Pub. L. 98–547

children.label: a
children.text: Whoever receives, possesses, conceals, stores, barters, sells, or disposes of any motor vehicle, vessel, or aircraft, which has crossed a State or United States boundary after being stolen, knowing the same to have been stolen, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than 10 years, or both.

children.label: b
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “State” includes a State of the United States, the District of Columbia, and any commonwealth, territory, or possession of the United States.
